1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are not the same entity.
1.14: Firms may act if a member is the seller provided a separate partner (not related to the seller) acts for the lender.
There must be no conflict of interest.
Actions must align with professional guidelines.
1.15: Firms may act if a member is the borrower provided a separate partner (not related to the borrower) acts for the lender.
There must be no conflict of interest.
Actions must align with professional guidelines.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor firm name and address is not required.
3.1.4: Lending is prohibited if the seller does not have legal representation.
3.1.5: No alternative identity verification documents are accepted.

3.2.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor firm name and address is required.
4.5a: Contact point for re-inspection: Refer to section 1.11a.
4.5b: Certificate of title destination: Refer to section 1.11a.
Only unqualified certificates of title are accepted.
Certificates must be submitted in final form with copies of deeds of guarantee and ILA certificates.
4.1: Valuation reports are provided by the lender for the lender's benefit only.

5.1.1: Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions where the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months are prohibited.
Prohibition includes transactions where the seller is an entity controlled by the borrower or a related company director/shareholder.
Remortgages within 6 months of purchase are prohibited unless the borrower is already the registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports should not be sent unless they identify impacts on security.
5.4.5: Personal local authority searches are accepted if the firm is Search Code subscribed and CoPSO registered.
An unqualified Certificate of Title must be provided.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for remortgages or SPV transfers where the individuals involved remain unchanged.
Insurance is only for local authority searches.
Insurance is prohibited for transactions of £1 million or more.
Insurance is prohibited for Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs).
Insurance is prohibited for Multi-Unit Freehold Blocks (MUBs).
5.5.3a: Contact point for reporting breaches: Refer to section 1.11a.
5.5.3b: Original/copy of planning permission is not required unless requested.
5.5.3c: Original/copy of building regulation consents is not required unless requested.
5.5.3d: Original/copy of certificates of lawful use/development is not required unless requested.
5.5.4: Properties with restrictions affecting value/marketability: Refer to section 1.11a.
Only unqualified certificates of title are accepted.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they do not exceed 10% of the total floor area (excluding coach houses).
Indemnity insurance is mandatory for flying freeholds.
5.7.1c: Report flying freehold or freehold flat titles to section 1.11a.
5.8.1: Occupancy by the borrower or their relative is prohibited for buy-to-let mortgages.
A charge over the freehold is required if the borrower or a related entity controls the freehold.
5.8.5: Two-flat leasehold building security accepted if repairing obligations are split at the first floor joist level.
Lease must have 85+ years remaining with nominal ground rent.
The freehold must be charged to the lender.
Freehold and lease must be transferred together to the same owner.
Access and maintenance legal arrangements must be established.
5.10.1: Contact point for use restrictions: Refer to section 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absent or insolvent landlords.
5.14.17a: Building safety documentation should not be sent unless specifically requested via section 5.14.17b.
5.14.17b: Flats require confirmation of a current Fire Risk Assessment (FRA).
Lending is prohibited if an FRA is required but unavailable/expired.
Report FRA results identifying 'substantial', 'intolerable', or 'extreme harm' risks, including cost and timescale details.
EWS1 forms are not required for buildings completed on or after 1 January 2020.
EWS1 forms are required for older buildings if specified in the valuation report.
Remediation liabilities under Building Safety Act 2022 must be reported immediately; lending will not proceed.
5.14.17c: Building safety instructions apply to remortgages.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 85 years at mortgage commencement.
Minimum unexpired lease term: 50 years at the scheduled end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: Ground rent reporting regarding Assured Tenancy status is not required for leases over 21 years due to Renters’ Rights Act 2025.
Onerous ground rents must be reported, including doubling < 25 years or those linked to capital value.
Leases/extensions granted after 22 June 2022 must comply with the Leasehold Reform (Ground Rent) Act 2022.
5.14.10: Report service charges exceeding £5,000 or 1% of property value (whichever is lower).
Report significant planned increases or works, including list of works, cost, timescale, and borrower contribution.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Ground rent/service charge receipts do not need to be sent but must be verified as clear by the conveyancer.
5.14.13: Receipted notice of service is not required to be sent.
5.15.2a: Indemnity insurance accepted for historic/uncollected rent charges (12+ years).
Estate rent charges accepted if Section 121 of the Law of Property Act 1925 is excluded.
Estate rent charges accepted if 28 days' notice to the mortgagee is required before enforcement.
Estate rent charges accepted if the owner is a resident-owned management company.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms should be retained on file, not sent.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association of the management company do not need to be sent.
5.16.2: Search entry issues: Refer to section 1.11a.
5.17.5a: Documents signed by attorneys are not accepted.
All documents must be signed by the borrower.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations for power of attorney are not applicable.
5.19.1: Lending is prohibited for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership properties.
5.20.1: Solar panel leases must meet UK Finance minimum requirements.

6.6.3: Tenancy agreements should not be sent.
Accepted tenancies: AST (England) or SOC (Wales) max 36 months, rent <£100k per annum.
Corporate tenancies: Max 5 years, wholly residential, no care services.
Shared households/Student lets: Max 5 years, residential only, no assignment.
Leases > 12 months require an unconditional right to terminate or termination upon receiver appointment.
Holiday lets (Airbnb), protected tenancies, and agricultural tenancies are prohibited.
6.6.4: Lending on HMOs is permitted per the terms of the mortgage offer.
6.7.1: Accepted warranties: NHBC, Zurich Municipal, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plans, Buildzone, LABC, ICW, Q Policy, Checkmate, GHW, Advantage HCI, Protek, Aedis/Homeproof, ABC+, Ark Group, Build Assure.
Warranties must involve periodic construction checks; retrospective warranties are prohibited.
6.7.2: New home warranty documentation should not be sent; forward to the borrower.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes should not be sent unless requested.
6.7.4: Properties without warranties must have construction monitored by a qualified professional consultant.

6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates should not be sent.
